tasks.task_name: Find warm backpacking destination (1-2 night, mid-March)
tasks.first_domino: Open Google and search 'warm backpacking March 14-15 weekend'
tasks.hook_line: March 14 is 4 days out — where can you actually camp in warm weather this time of year?
tasks.reward: Unlocks flights and gear planning — the biggest blockers between now and the trip
nuggets: Southern Utah averages 60°F mid-March
Big Bend TX allows dispersed camping
Red River Gorge KY 55°F, no permit needed
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find a warm backpacking destination for March 14-15, 2026 (this weekend). I need a 1-2 night trip somewhere warm enough for comfortable camping. I'm flying from a major US city and need to get back to Chicago by March 16. I want specific location recommendations with: average March temps, permit requirements, trailhead details, and whether I can fly there easily. Focus on accessible locations — I'm renting gear from REI so this is a beginner-friendly trip.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Flights and search from your city to destination
tasks.hook_line: 3 days out — flights for weekend travel get expensive fast or disappear entirely
tasks.reward: Locks in the trip dates and removes the biggest logistical question mark
nuggets: Weekend flights book up 5-7 days out
Southwest flies to most warm destinations
Thursday night departure avoids Friday surge pricing
tasks.system_prompt: Help me book flights for a backpacking trip departing March 14, 2026 (Thursday evening or Friday morning) and returning March 16, 2026 (Sunday). I'm flying from [YOUR_CITY] to [DESTINATION]. I need to connect to Chicago on March 16 evening. Find me flight options on Google Flights, prioritize: direct routes, reasonable departure times (after 5pm Thu or before 10am Fri), and lowest cost. Include layover considerations for the return to Chicago leg.

tasks.task_name: Book flight from backpacking to Chicago
tasks.first_domino: Check flight times from backpacking city to Chicago on March 16
tasks.hook_line: Chicago flight is already booked — this is the last leg to connect the whole trip
tasks.reward: Closes out travel logistics completely, leaving only gear to sort
nuggets: Arrive Chicago by 8pm for nightlife meetup
Sunday evening flights less crowded
Same-airline booking simplifies baggage
tasks.system_prompt: Help me book a flight from [BACKPACKING_DESTINATION] to Chicago on March 16, 2026 (Sunday). I need to arrive in Chicago by 8pm to meet up with friends for Mariano's birthday celebration. Find flight options that: arrive O'Hare or Midway by 8pm, minimize layovers, and ideally connect with my existing Chicago-to-home flight if possible. I'm carrying a backpack so luggage isn't a concern.

tasks.task_name: Figure out REI gear rentals (backpack, tent, sleeping bag)
tasks.first_domino: Go to REI.com and search 'backpacking gear rental'
tasks.hook_line: REI rents full backpacking kits — you might not need to buy anything at all
tasks.reward: Answers the gear question without a $500+ purchase for a one-time trip
nuggets: REI Adventure Kits: $75/weekend all gear
Pick up Thu evening, return Mon morning
Includes tent, bag, pad, backpack, stove
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out REI's gear rental program for a 1-2 night backpacking trip March 14-16, 2026. I need: backpack, tent (1-2 person), sleeping bag (warm enough for 50-60°F nights), sleeping pad, and ideally a stove. Find: rental pricing, what's included in kits vs individual rentals, pickup/return logistics, and whether my local REI has availability. I've never rented gear before so explain how the process works.

tasks.first_domino: Text Mariano's close friends: 'What's he into these days?'
tasks.hook_line: What do you get someone turning 30 when you don't know their current hobbies?
tasks.reward: Avoids showing up empty-handed to a milestone birthday
nuggets: 30th birthdays: quality over novelty
Chicago-themed gifts if he's local
$50-100 sweet spot for friend birthdays
tasks.system_prompt: Help me brainstorm gift ideas for Mariano's 30th birthday. I'm attending his celebration in Chicago March 19, 2026. I don't know his current interests well — we haven't been in close touch. I want something thoughtful but not overly personal. Budget $50-150. Ideas should be: milestone-appropriate (turning 30), not generic, and ideally something he wouldn't buy himself. Include a mix of experiences, practical items, and conversation-starters.

tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.first_domino: Google 'eyeglasses stores Kyoto English-speaking JINS Zoff'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto is stop 1 — knock out glasses shopping early and enjoy the rest of the trip
tasks.reward: Gets the practical task out of the way so you can focus on temples and food
nuggets: JINS Kyoto Kawaramachi open 11am-8pm daily
Zoff near Kyoto Station has English staff
Glasses ready same-day for standard prescriptions
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto that are good for English-speaking tourists. I'm planning to buy 4 pairs of prescription glasses during my March 25-27 Kyoto visit. I need: store names (JINS, Zoff, etc), locations in central Kyoto, opening hours, whether they have English-speaking staff, and typical turnaround time for prescription glasses. Also tell me if I should bring anything specific (prescription format, paperwork, etc).

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Email insurance provider: subject 'International eyewear reimbursement policy'
tasks.hook_line: Can you actually get 4 pairs reimbursed, or are you about to spend $800 out of pocket?
tasks.reward: Know exactly what you're paying vs what insurance covers before you shop
nuggets: Most US vision plans cover 1 pair/year
Keep all receipts in English for reimbursement
Prescription format: sphere, cylinder, axis, PD
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out insurance and prescription requirements for buying eyeglasses in Japan. I have US vision insurance and want to buy 4 pairs of glasses during my March 23-April 7 trip. Questions: (1) What's the reimbursement process for international eyewear purchases? (2) What receipt/documentation format do I need? (3) How should I get my prescription formatted for use in Japan? (4) Can I submit multiple pairs for reimbursement or am I limited to 1/year? Draft an email template I can send to my insurance provider.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'top temples Kyoto walking distance'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are actually worth waking up early for?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left on the itinerary
nuggets: Fushimi Inari free entry, open 24hrs
Arashiyama bamboo grove best before 9am
Philosopher's Path: 2km walk, 10+ temples
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, and good food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with: opening hours, entry fees, Google Maps links, and a suggested walking route. Include a mix of major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama) and less touristy spots. Also factor in time for eyeglasses shopping and lunch breaks. We prefer walking over taxis.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'Takayama day trips hiking villages March weather'
tasks.hook_line: Takayama is the mountain town — what's hiding in the Japanese Alps?
tasks.reward: Fills in the middle section of the trip between Kyoto and Tokyo
nuggets: Shirakawa-go village: UNESCO site, 1hr bus
Takayama Old Town: preserved Edo-era streets
Hida Folk Village: outdoor museum, 30min walk
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities in Takayama, Japan for 2 days (March 28-29, 2026). It's a mountain town in the Japanese Alps. Dana and I like: hiking, traditional villages, local food, and outdoor experiences. I want recommendations for: day trips (Shirakawa-go?), local walking routes, food specialties (Hida beef?), and whether we need a car or if buses work. Include opening hours, transportation logistics, and typical March weather (still snowy?). We prefer active itineraries over museums.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and mark all Tokyo neighborhoods we want to visit
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is massive — which neighborhoods actually deliver on the vibe you're imagining?
tasks.reward: Completes the entire Japan itinerary — nothing left to plan
nuggets: Shibuya nightlife peaks Thu-Sat after 10pm
Tsukiji Outer Market best 8-11am
teamLab Borderless: book 2 weeks ahead
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 5 days in Tokyo (March 30 - April 3, 2026). Traveling with Dana. We like: street food, nightlife, outdoor spaces, unique experiences, and shopping (fountain pens, watches, stationery). We've been to Tokyo before so skip the obvious tourist traps. I want neighborhood recommendations with: what makes each one special, best times to visit, specific shops or restaurants worth seeking out, and a loose day-by-day structure. Include a mix of day activities and evening plans. We're staying in central Tokyo and happy to use trains.

tasks.first_domino: Clone the Cerebro repo and run 'npm install'
tasks.hook_line: What's actually blocking local setup — missing docs, environment config, or something deeper?
tasks.reward: Unblocks all future Cerebro work — you can't develop what you can't run
nuggets: Check README for required environment variables
Common issue: database connection string format
May need Docker for local services
tasks.system_prompt: Help me get the Cerebro developer portal running locally. It's an internal tool for engineers. I need to: (1) clone the repo, (2) figure out dependencies and environment setup, (3) get it running on localhost. Walk me through the typical setup process for a Node.js developer portal. What are the most common blockers? (missing env vars, database setup, auth configuration). I'm comfortable with command line and have Docker available. Give me a step-by-step troubleshooting checklist.

recommended_slots.reason: 3 days out, unblocks all other trip logistics, low effort research perfect for ramp_up energy
Japan trip is 13 days out, eyeglasses stores research is quick win that enables Kyoto planning
REI gear research is low context cost, 20min task, pairs well with destination search
